content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 14, 1991


Peter G. DeMauro, General Counsel
Employment Training Panel
P.O. Box 826880, MIC 64
Sacramento, CA 94280-0001

Re: Your Request for Advice
    Our File No. I-90-769

Dear Mr. DeMauro:

     You have sought advice on behalf of the State of California
Employment Training Panel, generally regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"),1 and
specifically with reference to the "rule of necessity" provisions
as they pertain to an otherwise disqualified appointed panel
member.

     We are unable to provide you with the advice you seek.
Although the Commission does provide written advice to persons
with questions about their duties under the Act (Section
8311(b)), the advice provided is prospective in its application
and is limited to the provisions of the Act. The Commission does
not provide advice with respect to past conduct or matters dealing
with other statutes. (Regulation 18329, copy enclosed.)

     Based upon the facts provided in your letter of December 21,
1990, the provisions of the Act about which you have sought advice
concerned a proposal pending before the Employment Training Panel
at its monthly meetings in November and December, 1990. Only four
of the seven Panel members were present at the November meeting;
of the four, one member had a disqualifying conflict and abstained
from the vote on the matter. At the Panel Chair's suggestion, the
matter was revisited at the Panel's December meeting, and you
indicated that "the full Panel" voted to disapprove the proposal.

     With respect to the November meeting, you inquired whether:
(1) the Panel member otherwise disqualified because of a
conflict of interest could participate under the "rule of
necessity" so as to form a quorum, then abstain from voting

_________________________
1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
   are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
   regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
   18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
   Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. I-90-769
Page 2

without affecting the quorum; (2) a quorum was established without
the disqualified Panel member, since the absent Panel members were
not otherwise disqualified from voting; and (3) the vote on the
proposal - two affirmative, one negative - and the abstention by
the Panel member disqualified, nevertheless constituted denial of
the proposal.

We cannot provide you with advice because the issue serving
as the basis for your inquiry constitutes conduct which has
already occurred. Moreover, questions concerning what does or
does not constitute a quorum or an affirmative vote on a proposal
are not questions which arise under the Act.

In order to provide you with some general guidance should a
similar situation arise in the future, we draw your attention to
Regulation 18701 (copy enclosed). Providing a limited basis in
which an otherwise disqualified official is permitted to
participate in a decision, the regulation may

          Not be construed to allow a member of any
          public agency, who is otherwise disqualified
          under Government Code Section 87100, to vote
          if a quorum can be convened of other members
          of the agency who are not disqualified under
          Government Code Section 87100, whether or not
          such other members are actually present at the
          time of the disqualification.

                    Regulation 18701(c)(2) (emphasis added)

Also, please find enclosed copies of the Phillips Advice Letter,
No. I-82-111, and the Freeman Advice Letter, No. A-90-525. The
Phillips letter concludes that a disqualified official does not
"count" for purposes of a quorum calculation. The Freeman letter
concludes that an otherwise disqualified official under Regulation
18701 who is permitted to participate in a decision is generally
permitted to do so fully.

I hope this letter has provided you with guidance that can be
useful for future purposes. If you have any further questions
regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
    Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken

Enclosures
